name: Heirloom Raisin Muffins
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine raisins and water in a saucepan. Bring to a boil, reduce heat and cover.
2. Simmer for 20 minutes. Drain the raisins, reserving the liquid.
3. Add enough of the reserved liquid to make 1/2 cup. Cool well.
4. In a bowl, cream together butter and sugar using an electric mixer at medium speed until light and fluffy.
5. Add eggs, beating for an additional 2 minutes.
6. Sift together flour and baking powder.
7. Alternately add the flour mixture with 1/2 cup of the reserved raisin liquid into the butter mixture, mixing well after each addition.
8. Stir in the drained raisins.
9. Spoon batter into greased muffin cups, filling them about 2/3 full.
10. Bake in a preheated oven to 400°F (200°C) for 18 minutes or until golden brown.
11. Serve hot with homemade jam or jelly.
